source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/04_제품별 인허가 진행 문서/01_HAD1717MC(MG)/▶ CE MDD/■ 3. 3차 CE TCF 보안사항 제출 (12월10일)/03. Risk_Management/ARMR-FMEA-0-001 Risk Table 19.9.17.xlsx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management table mapping ISO 14971 and IEC 60601-1 clauses to hazards, risk controls, residual risk acceptability, and verification evidence for product HAD/A/F 1417-1717.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports CE MDD technical file risk management evidence by documenting hazards, risk estimation, control measures, implementation evidence, residual risks, and acceptability.
keyPoints: Essential performance includes image acquisition within 5 seconds and acquired images without two Line Adjacent, Line Block, or No Display.
Risk controls address leakage current, user recognition errors, overheating, component fixation, software degradation of function, connector misuse, and electromagnetic waves.

Residual risk outcomes are generally recorded as Acceptable after controls and verification evidence such as user manuals, work instructions, test reports, inspection standards, and software validation reports.

Many IEC 60601-1 clauses are marked N/A because the product has no patient contact parts, no lithium batteries, no moving parts, no diagnostic or therapeutic radiation, and no sterilization requirement.

entities.name: Software validation report
entities.evidence: software for centrifuge and control is applied: software validation report

concepts.name: Risk Management
concepts.evidence: 4.3 Identification of hazards, 4.4 Estimation of the risk(s) for each hazardous situation, 5. Risk Evaluation, 6.2 Risk Control option analysis

concepts.name: Essential Performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance. - Must be able to acquire image within 5 seconds - The acquired image should not have two Line Adjacent, Line Block, or No Display.

concepts.name: Single Fault Condition
concepts.evidence: The single fault condition described in 13.2 applies.

concepts.name: Leakage Current
concepts.evidence: RM-02, leakage current, Can deliver unacceptable current to the patient or operator in a single fault condition, Electrical hazards

concepts.name: Protective Packaging Marking
concepts.evidence: Operating conditions displayed on the outer packaging Temperature, Humidity, Pressure

concepts.name: Safety Signs
concepts.evidence: ISO 3864-1: 2002 Cover corresponding to Clause 7 and ISO 7010: 20030W001 Symbol Application

concepts.name: Electromagnetic Compatibility
concepts.evidence: Electromagnetic compatibility of ME equipment and ME systems

evidence.claim: The document is a risk table structured around ISO 14971 clauses for hazard identification, risk estimation, risk evaluation, risk control, and residual risk acceptability.
evidence.evidenceText: "ISO 14971 Clause" ... "4.3 Identification of hazards" ... "4.4 Estimation of the risk(s)" ... "5. Risk Evaluation" ... "6.2 Risk Control option analysis" ... "6.4 Residual risk evaluation"

evidence.claim: The table maps risk management questions to IEC 60601-1 clauses and requirements.
evidence.evidenceText: No, IEC 60601-1 Clause, IEC60601-1 Requirements, Questions for identifying characteristics

evidence.claim: Essential performance includes acquiring an image within 5 seconds and preventing two Line Adjacent, Line Block, or No Display in acquired images.
evidence.evidenceText: Must be able to acquire image within 5 seconds - The acquired image should not have two Line Adjacent, Line Block, or No Display.

evidence.claim: The essential performance risk RM-01 identifies operational hazards leading to incorrect measurement, initially unacceptable with risk 9 and reduced to acceptable with residual risk 1.
evidence.evidenceText: RM-01, Operational Hazards, Measurement in a fault condition, Incorrect measurement, 3,3,9,Unacceptable ... 1,1,1,Acceptable

evidence.claim: The product is stated to have no patient-contact part.
evidence.evidenceText: This product do not have any comtact to patient part.

evidence.claim: Single fault condition assessment includes electrical single fault conditions and overload protection using a FUSE rated 3.15AH / 250V at the AC power input terminal.
evidence.evidenceText: 13.2.2. Electrical single fault condition ... 13.2.13 Overload -> In case of overloading inside the system, a protection device using FUSE (3.15AH / 250V) is installed at the AC power input terminal

evidence.claim: Leakage current risk RM-02 is controlled by approved AC-DC transformer, AC cable, and fuse, with electrical safety testing during shipment inspection.
evidence.evidenceText: Designed to use approved AC-DC transformer, AC cable, fuse to prevent leakage current ... Electrical safety test is carried out during shipment inspection

evidence.claim: Protective packaging marking includes operating conditions on the outer packaging for temperature, humidity, and pressure.
evidence.evidenceText: Operating conditions displayed on the outer packaging Temperature, Humidity, Pressure

evidence.claim: The product is stated not to use lithium batteries or fuel cells.
evidence.evidenceText: This product do not use lithium batteries or fuel cell.

evidence.claim: FUSE and AC power cables are stated to be user replaceable, and the user manual describes specified cable use and fuse specifications and replacement methods.
evidence.evidenceText: FUSE and AC power cables are user replaceable ... Use only the specified cable. Describe the FUSE specifications and replacement methods available in the product exterior and user manual.

evidence.claim: Thermal insulation risk RM-08 is controlled by using flame-retardant insulators and verified through cable drawing, connector, external raw material data, and import inspection standard.
evidence.evidenceText: RM-08, Electromagnetic energy / thermal energy, Insulator may be damaged by heat ... Designed using flame-retardant insulators ... Cable drawing, connector, external raw material data, import inspection standard

evidence.claim: Component movement risk RM-09 concerns PCB, PCB components, AC-DC transformer, and AC filter fixed inside the product.
evidence.evidenceText: Inside the product, PCB, PCB components, AC-DC transformer and AC filter are fixed.

evidence.claim: The product is stated not to be associated with moving parts.
evidence.evidenceText: this product is not associated with moving parts

evidence.claim: The product is stated not to produce diagnostic or therapeutic radiation.
evidence.evidenceText: It is not a product that causes factors for diagnostic or therapeutic radiation

evidence.claim: Cleaning is limited to the main body and sensor part with a dry cloth, with no other risk stated.
evidence.evidenceText: The main body and the sensor part can be cleaned with a dry cloth. There is no other risk.

evidence.claim: The product is stated not to require sterilization.
evidence.evidenceText: this product do not require sterillization.

evidence.claim: Software-related degradation of function risks RM-11 through RM-17 are controlled by software design and software validation, with software validation report as evidence.
evidence.evidenceText: RM-11, Degradation of function ... Software Design to Achieve Essential Performance, Software validation ... Software validation report

evidence.claim: Connector misuse risk RM-18 covers removable AC power cable, data cable, LAN cable, and trigger cable, controlled by connector design and user manual instructions.
evidence.evidenceText: AC power cable, data cable, LAN cable, trigger cable can be removed without tools ... Designed not to be mixed with other connectors ... Instruction manual to connect the specified cable

evidence.claim: Electromagnetic compatibility risk RM-19 states the product is used in general practice rooms and may affect other ME devices through electromagnetic effects.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is used in general practice rooms and it can affect electromagnetic effects on various ME devices in the medical room.

evidence.claim: Electromagnetic wave risk RM-19 is recorded as acceptable both before and after applying electromagnetic hazards to design, with EMC Test report as evidence.
evidence.evidenceText: RM-19, electromagnetic waves, The performance of other devices is degraded due to electromagnetic waves., electrical hazard, 1,1,1,Acceptable ... EMC Test report ... 1,1,1,Acceptable

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not explicitly provided in the source text; filename contains 19.9.17 but this was not treated as a document date.
Revision or version is not explicitly provided; ARMR-FMEA-0-001 appears in the filename but is not clearly identified as a revision.
Authority is ambiguous: sourcePath indicates CE MDD, while authoritySignal is general and the table mainly cites standards rather than a named authority.
Source text has OCR or extraction quality issues, including misspellings such as harzadous, hazzards, comtact, faliures, and sterillization.

Possible conflicting product evidence: the product signal is HAD/A/F 1417-1717 and essential performance refers to image acquisition, while several software rows state software for centrifuge and control is applied.
Some standards references appear malformed or ambiguous, including ISO 7010: 20030W001 and IEC60601-1-14.
